Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: Covers hash compression concepts including modular arithmetic, prime number table sizing, and folding techniques (shift and boundary), serving as the navigational outline that guides learners through the sequence of topics in the module.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A topic outline covering hash compression fundamentals—including how large key spaces are mapped to bounded index ranges—intended to guide learners through the module's key concepts and vocabulary before diving into specific techniques.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Hash Compression
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces hash functions and hash compression, explaining how keys are converted into numeric table indices and why this process is fundamental to data structures like hash tables used in everyday applications such as phone contacts and compilers.

Artifacts.Name: Modular Arithmetic as a Compression Strategy
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how modular arithmetic (the division method) is used as a compression strategy to map arbitrary keys into valid hash table indices, covering its mechanics, advantages, and practical considerations for achieving efficient average-case performance.

Artifacts.Name: Folding and Other Compression Techniques
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers folding and other compression techniques—such as digit folding and shift folding—that break large keys like social security numbers or product codes into smaller parts and combine them to produce a hash table index within a manageable range.

Artifacts.Name: Distribution Properties of Hash Functions
Artifacts.Description: This reading examines the distribution properties of hash functions, explaining what uniform distribution means, why it matters for minimizing collisions, and how to evaluate whether a hash function spreads keys evenly across a table's available slots.

Artifacts.Name: Comparing and Selecting Compression Methods
Artifacts.Description: This reading compares multiple hash compression methods—such as modular, MAD, and bit-masking approaches—discussing their trade-offs in terms of distribution quality, computational cost, and suitability for different data types to guide method selection.

Artifacts.Name: Comparing and Selecting Compression Methods
Artifacts.Description: This reading compares multiple hash compression methods—such as modular, MAD, and bit-masking approaches—discussing their trade-offs in terms of distribution quality, computational cost, and suitability for different data types to guide method selection.
